The following chunk visualises epsilon squared effect sizes from the Kruskal-Wallis tests computed across all three analytical dimensions. Epsilon squared measures the proportion of variance in each feature attributable to source condition, providing a model-independent measure of feature-outcome association.

```{r effect_size_comparison}
# combine epsilon squared values from all three analytical dimensions
effect_sizes <- bind_rows(
  # dimension 1 — stylometric
  kw_results |>
    select(feature, epsilon_sq) |>
    mutate(dimension = "Dimension 1"),
  # dimension 2 — affective register
  kw_results_d2 |>
    select(feature, epsilon_sq) |>
    mutate(dimension = "Dimension 2"),
  # dimension 3 — social identity construction
  tibble(
    feature    = c("strong_modal_rate", "weak_modal_rate",
                   "imperative_rate", "total_politeness"),
    epsilon_sq = c(round(es_strong$effsize, 4),
                   round(es_weak$effsize, 4),
                   round(es_imperative$effsize, 4),
                   round(es_politeness$effsize, 4)),
    dimension  = "Dimension 3"
  )
) |>
  mutate(
    feature = recode(feature,
                     "mtld"                 = "MTLD",
                     "flesch_kincaid_grade" = "FK Grade",
                     "passive_voice_rate"   = "Passive Voice Rate",
                     "function_word_rate"   = "Function Word Rate",
                     "exclamation_rate"     = "Exclamation Rate",
                     "anger"                = "Anger",
                     "anticipation"         = "Anticipation",
                     "disgust"              = "Disgust",
                     "fear"                 = "Fear",
                     "joy"                  = "Joy",
                     "sadness"              = "Sadness",
                     "surprise"             = "Surprise",
                     "trust"                = "Trust",
                     "negative"             = "Negative Valence",
                     "positive"             = "Positive Valence",
                     "sentimentr_score"     = "Sentimentr Score",
                     "strong_modal_rate"    = "Strong Modality",
                     "weak_modal_rate"      = "Weak Modality",
                     "imperative_rate"      = "Imperative Rate",
                     "total_politeness"     = "Politeness Score"
    ),
    feature = fct_reorder(feature, epsilon_sq)
  )

p_effect_sizes <- ggplot(effect_sizes,
                         aes(x = feature, y = epsilon_sq, fill = dimension)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.85) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Dimension 1" = "#CC3311",
      "Dimension 2" = "#DDAA33",
      "Dimension 3" = "#004488"
    ),
    name = "Dimension"
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Feature Association with Source Condition (Epsilon Squared)",
    x     = NULL,
    y     = "Epsilon squared (Kruskal-Wallis)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", colour = "#1F4E79"),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

p_effect_sizes
```

#### 5.10.2 Single Features Classifiers

The following chunk trains a separate random forest on each feature individually using 10-fold cross-validation, assessing each feature's standalone discriminative power independent of the full model context.

```{r single_feature_classifiers}
single_feature_accuracy <- map_dfr(
  names(model_data)[names(model_data) != "source"],
  function(feat) {
    set.seed(42)
    feat_data  <- model_data |> select(source, all_of(feat)) |> drop_na()
    n_features <- ncol(feat_data) - 1
    # mtry cannot exceed number of features — set to 1 for single-feature models
    rf <- train(
      source ~ .,
      data      = feat_data,
      method    = "ranger",
      trControl = cv_control,
      metric    = "Accuracy",
      tuneGrid  = expand.grid(
        mtry          = min(c(4, 6, 8), n_features),
        splitrule     = "gini",
        min.node.size = 5
      )
    )
    tibble(
      feature  = feat,
      accuracy = round(max(rf$results$Accuracy), 3)
    )
  }
)

single_feature_accuracy <- single_feature_accuracy |>
  arrange(desc(accuracy)) |>
  mutate(
    feature = recode(feature,
                     "mtld"                 = "MTLD",
                     "flesch_kincaid_grade" = "FK Grade",
                     "passive_voice_rate"   = "Passive Voice Rate",
                     "function_word_rate"   = "Function Word Rate",
                     "exclamation_rate"     = "Exclamation Rate",
                     "anger"                = "Anger",
                     "anticipation"         = "Anticipation",
                     "disgust"              = "Disgust",
                     "fear"                 = "Fear",
                     "joy"                  = "Joy",
                     "sadness"              = "Sadness",
                     "surprise"             = "Surprise",
                     "trust"                = "Trust",
                     "negative"             = "Negative Valence",
                     "positive"             = "Positive Valence",
                     "sentimentr_score"     = "Sentimentr Score",
                     "strong_modal_rate"    = "Strong Modality",
                     "weak_modal_rate"      = "Weak Modality",
                     "imperative_rate"      = "Imperative Rate",
                     "total_politeness"     = "Politeness Score"
    ),
    feature = fct_reorder(feature, accuracy)
  )

print(single_feature_accuracy)


p_single_feature <- single_feature_accuracy |>
  mutate(
    dimension = case_when(
      feature %in% c("MTLD", "FK Grade", "Passive Voice Rate",
                     "Function Word Rate", "Exclamation Rate") ~ "Dimension 1",
      feature %in% c("Disgust", "Fear", "Positive Valence", "Sadness",
                     "Trust", "Negative Valence", "Sentimentr Score",
                     "Surprise", "Anger", "Anticipation", "Joy") ~ "Dimension 2",
      TRUE ~ "Dimension 3"
    )
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = feature, y = accuracy, fill = dimension)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.85) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Dimension 1" = "#CC3311",
      "Dimension 2" = "#DDAA33",
      "Dimension 3" = "#004488"
    ),
    name = "Dimension"
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.343, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  annotate("text", x = 1, y = 0.36, label = "Baseline (34.3%)",
           colour = "grey50", size = 3, hjust = 0) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Single-Feature Classification Accuracy (Random Forest, 10-Fold CV)",
    x     = NULL,
    y     = "Accuracy"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", colour = "#1F4E79"),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

p_single_feature
```

#### 5.10.3 Permutation Test

The following chunk implements a manual permutation importance analysis. For each feature in turn, the feature's values are randomly shuffled across 50 permutations and the full model is retrained and evaluated. The mean accuracy drop and standard deviation across permutations are reported. Error bars show variability across permutations.

Note that this chunk is computationally expensive - it trains 1,000 models (50 permutations × 20 features) using 5-fold cross-validation. Runtime will vary depending on hardware but may take upwards of two hours depending on hardware capabilities.

```{r permutation_importance}
set.seed(42)

n_permutations  <- 50
# baseline accuracy from the full 10-fold CV model
baseline_accuracy <- mean(rf_model$resample$Accuracy)

permutation_results <- map_dfr(
  names(model_data)[names(model_data) != "source"],
  function(feat) {
    perm_accuracies <- map_dbl(seq_len(n_permutations), function(i) {
      set.seed(i)
      permuted_data <- model_data |>
        mutate(!!feat := sample(!!sym(feat)))
      rf_perm <- train(
        source ~ .,
        data      = permuted_data,
        method    = "ranger",
        trControl = trainControl(
          method          = "cv",
          number          = 5,
          classProbs      = TRUE,
          summaryFunction = multiClassSummary
        ),
        metric   = "Accuracy",
        tuneGrid = expand.grid(
          mtry          = 4,
          splitrule     = "gini",
          min.node.size = 5
        )
      )
      mean(rf_perm$resample$Accuracy)
    })
    tibble(
      feature       = feat,
      mean_perm_acc = round(mean(perm_accuracies), 3),
      accuracy_drop = round(baseline_accuracy - mean(perm_accuracies), 3),
      sd_perm_acc   = round(sd(perm_accuracies), 3)
    )
  }
)

permutation_results <- permutation_results |>
  arrange(desc(accuracy_drop)) |>
  mutate(
    feature = recode(feature,
                     "mtld"                 = "MTLD",
                     "flesch_kincaid_grade" = "FK Grade",
                     "passive_voice_rate"   = "Passive Voice Rate",
                     "function_word_rate"   = "Function Word Rate",
                     "exclamation_rate"     = "Exclamation Rate",
                     "anger"                = "Anger",
                     "anticipation"         = "Anticipation",
                     "disgust"              = "Disgust",
                     "fear"                 = "Fear",
                     "joy"                  = "Joy",
                     "sadness"              = "Sadness",
                     "surprise"             = "Surprise",
                     "trust"                = "Trust",
                     "negative"             = "Negative Valence",
                     "positive"             = "Positive Valence",
                     "sentimentr_score"     = "Sentimentr Score",
                     "strong_modal_rate"    = "Strong Modality",
                     "weak_modal_rate"      = "Weak Modality",
                     "imperative_rate"      = "Imperative Rate",
                     "total_politeness"     = "Politeness Score"
    ),
    feature   = fct_reorder(feature, accuracy_drop),
    dimension = case_when(
      feature %in% c("MTLD", "FK Grade", "Passive Voice Rate",
                     "Function Word Rate", "Exclamation Rate") ~ "Dimension 1",
      feature %in% c("Disgust", "Fear", "Positive Valence", "Sadness",
                     "Trust", "Negative Valence", "Sentimentr Score",
                     "Surprise", "Anger", "Anticipation", "Joy") ~ "Dimension 2",
      TRUE ~ "Dimension 3"
    )
  )

print(permutation_results)

p_permutation <- ggplot(permutation_results,
                        aes(x = feature, y = accuracy_drop, fill = dimension)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = accuracy_drop - sd_perm_acc,
        ymax = accuracy_drop + sd_perm_acc),
    width = 0.3, colour = "#2E2E2E"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Dimension 1" = "#CC3311",
      "Dimension 2" = "#DDAA33",
      "Dimension 3" = "#004488"
    ),
    name = "Dimension"
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title    = "Feature Permutation Importance (Accuracy Drop)",
    subtitle = paste0("Baseline accuracy: ", round(baseline_accuracy, 3),
                      " | 50 permutations per feature | 5-fold CV"),
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Mean accuracy drop when feature is permuted"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", colour = "#1F4E79"),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(colour = "#666666"),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

p_permutation
```

#### 5.10.4 Ablation Analysis

The following chunk trains the random forest 20 times, removing one feature at a time, to assess how much accuracy drops when each feature is completely absent from the model. Unlike permutation importance, the model cannot compensate via correlated features since the feature is entirely unavailable.

```{r ablation_analysis}
set.seed(42)

all_features <- names(model_data)[names(model_data) != "source"]

ablation_results <- map_dfr(all_features, function(feat) {
  ablated_data <- model_data |> select(-all_of(feat))
  n_features   <- ncol(ablated_data) - 1
  
  rf_ablated <- train(
    source ~ .,
    data      = ablated_data,
    method    = "ranger",
    trControl = trainControl(
      method          = "cv",
      number          = 5,
      classProbs      = TRUE,
      summaryFunction = multiClassSummary
    ),
    metric   = "Accuracy",
    tuneGrid = expand.grid(
      mtry          = min(4, n_features),
      splitrule     = "gini",
      min.node.size = 5
    )
  )
  
  tibble(
    feature       = feat,
    ablated_acc   = round(mean(rf_ablated$resample$Accuracy), 3),
    accuracy_drop = round(baseline_accuracy - mean(rf_ablated$resample$Accuracy), 3)
  )
})

ablation_results <- ablation_results |>
  arrange(desc(accuracy_drop)) |>
  mutate(
    feature = recode(feature,
                     "mtld"                 = "MTLD",
                     "flesch_kincaid_grade" = "FK Grade",
                     "passive_voice_rate"   = "Passive Voice Rate",
                     "function_word_rate"   = "Function Word Rate",
                     "exclamation_rate"     = "Exclamation Rate",
                     "anger"                = "Anger",
                     "anticipation"         = "Anticipation",
                     "disgust"              = "Disgust",
                     "fear"                 = "Fear",
                     "joy"                  = "Joy",
                     "sadness"              = "Sadness",
                     "surprise"             = "Surprise",
                     "trust"                = "Trust",
                     "negative"             = "Negative Valence",
                     "positive"             = "Positive Valence",
                     "sentimentr_score"     = "Sentimentr Score",
                     "strong_modal_rate"    = "Strong Modality",
                     "weak_modal_rate"      = "Weak Modality",
                     "imperative_rate"      = "Imperative Rate",
                     "total_politeness"     = "Politeness Score"
    ),
    feature   = fct_reorder(feature, accuracy_drop),
    dimension = case_when(
      feature %in% c("MTLD", "FK Grade", "Passive Voice Rate",
                     "Function Word Rate", "Exclamation Rate") ~ "Dimension 1",
      feature %in% c("Disgust", "Fear", "Positive Valence", "Sadness",
                     "Trust", "Negative Valence", "Sentimentr Score",
                     "Surprise", "Anger", "Anticipation", "Joy") ~ "Dimension 2",
      TRUE ~ "Dimension 3"
    )
  )

print(ablation_results)

p_ablation <- ggplot(ablation_results,
                     aes(x = feature, y = accuracy_drop, fill = dimension)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.85) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Dimension 1" = "#CC3311",
      "Dimension 2" = "#DDAA33",
      "Dimension 3" = "#004488"
    ),
    name = "Dimension"
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title    = "Ablation Analysis: Accuracy Drop When Feature Removed",
    subtitle = paste0("Baseline accuracy: ", round(baseline_accuracy, 3),
                      " | 5-fold CV"),
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Accuracy drop when feature removed"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", colour = "#1F4E79"),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(colour = "#666666"),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

p_ablation
```
